cashflow.note: Monthly inflows, outflows and cumulative cash position. Quarterly rent payments hit Aug, Nov and Feb (May–Jul prepaid). For the full detailed model see the workbook.
cashflow.customDisabledHint: Lock the 2026 Performance forecast to populate the Custom scenario.

fixedCosts.note: Line items from rows 21–32 of the 2025 weekly P&L workbook. Monthly fees are entered GROSS — including any VAT charged by suppliers, matching how each line appears on invoices. Rent is auto-computed at 15% of turnover (contractual, not editable). Total flows to the Fixed Costs line in the cost donut; the model handles input-VAT reclaim automatically (output VAT − input VAT).
fixedCosts.rentFormula: Contractually {{pct}} of quarterly turnover. Auto-tracks the live 2026 forecast revenue ({{revenue}}). Move the Master Ticket Volume slider or any income lever to see this update.

officeCosts.note: Software subscriptions, AI tooling, accountant fees and director compensation. SaaS rates pulled from the 2025 weekly P&L where present, otherwise from current online pricing. All figures editable.
officeCosts.modelNote: This total flows to the cost donut as a new "Office & Admin" line. Lowering an item drops costs and lifts EBITDA in real time.

xero.name: Xero
xero.note: Cloud accounting software. £25/mo per the 2025 weekly P&L.

rotacloud.name: RotaCloud
rotacloud.note: Staff scheduling for 10 users. ~£40/mo at current per-user pricing.

claude.name: Claude AI
claude.note: Claude Pro subscription for ops/analysis. £20/mo current pricing.

google.name: Google Workspace
google.note: Email, Drive, calendar. £25/mo per the 2025 weekly P&L.

webhosting.name: Basic web hosting
webhosting.note: Simple venue website (menu, contact, info page). Plonk Golf owns the booking platform; this covers the venue's own static site only. Annual prepay.

amazonPrime.name: Amazon Prime
amazonPrime.note: Monthly Prime subscription for fast delivery on stock supplies, equipment and consumables. £8.99/mo at current pricing.

accounting.name: Accounting fees
accounting.note: Annual accountant fees — year-end statutory accounts, payroll, VAT returns.

director.name: Directors' salaries
director.note: Total budget for all directors' time — can be spread across multiple directors. PAYE / dividend mix at each director's discretion.

performance2025.ticketSource: Borough 2025 · Online portal (actual) vs Office/till (imputed at list price)
performance2025.officeNote: Status = external · imputed at list price
performance2025.monthlyTicket: Monthly Ticket Revenue — Online + Office Stacked

costCategories.fixedDesc: Rent (auto · 15% of turnover), rates, electricity, insurance, internet, PRS, maintenance
costCategories.vatDesc: Net VAT paid to HMRC — VAT collected minus VAT reclaimed on purchases
costCategories.googleDesc: 2025 historical Lithos/Google spend · under new IP & Licensing model all ad/SEO spend sits with Plonk Golf from 2026

performance2026.sliderDesc: Live readout of the 2026 forecast. Use the Sections below — Ticket Price · Income · Operating Costs · Fixed Costs · Wages · Office Costs — to model your 2026 plan. The slider, KPI tiles and scenario cards update as you go.
performance2026.lockTitle: Lock the forecast — capture current values and freeze all sliders. Locked values flow into the deck slides as the Custom scenario.
performance2026.unlockTitle: Unlock to edit again. Slides will revert to default scenarios until re-locked.
performance2026.lockedNote: Forecast locked — all editable inputs frozen. The locked values populate the Custom scenario card on Investment Summary and Investor Returns slides. Click Unlock to edit again.
performance2026.viewOnlyNote: View only — the 2026 Performance forecast is editable by the founder only (login code 888999). Any locked snapshot will populate the Custom scenario card on the deck slides.

costNotes.wagesDriven: Driven by the wage calculator below — sliders control the rate per role × 2026 hours
costNotes.fixed: Rent (15% of turnover) + rates, utilities, insurance, internet, PRS at +10% on 2025
costNotes.fixedDriven: Driven by the Fixed Costs section — 12 line items, each with a monthly slider to test vendor quotes
costNotes.office: Apps, AI, accountant fees and director's salary — driven by the Office Costs section below.
costNotes.plonkCommission: Commission paid to Plonk Golf on golf ticket revenue (online + office channels). Driven by the locked Commissions sliders on the IP & Licensing tab — booking fee excluded (goes to the bookings system, not Plonk).
costNotes.arcades: Operator cost = sum of (tokens × volume) across all SKUs × £0.325. Lower per-SKU tokens in the Ticket Price Maker below to cut this.
costNotes.vatComputed: Output VAT (revenue × 1/6) − reclaimed Input VAT — wages, arcades and food are zero-rated

scenarios.leverNote: These four levers also drive the main 2026 Performance slider

priceMaker.header: Ticket Price Maker · Per-SKU Unit Economics
priceMaker.note: Edit price and tokens per SKU. Cost per ticket = 16% rent/bills allocation + 20% VAT + tokens × £0.325. The master slider scales all SKU volumes proportionally based on the 2025 sales mix. Token totals flow back to the Arcades cost line.
priceMaker.masterSub: Scales all SKU volumes proportionally to 2025 sales mix
priceMaker.linkNote: Total token cost flows to the Arcades line in the cost donut. Changing tokens here moves EBITDA in real time. Income lines still come from the growth levers — matrix revenue is shown for unit-economics insight, not yet a live override.
